title: AIAM-1 Common Types
description: Shared type definitions for AIAM-1 schemas. All ID formats, timestamps, and reusable structures are defined here and $ref'd into identity_claim, intent_claim, and attestation_record schemas.

spec_version.description: AIAM-1 specification version this artifact was authored against (e.g., '0.1')

claim_id.description: Identity claim ID. Format: idc-<namespace>. Must be globally unique within the governance domain.

intent_id.description: Intent claim ID. Format: int-<namespace>. Must be globally unique — use UUIDs, hash-based IDs, or namespace+timestamp patterns to ensure uniqueness across deployments.

goal_id.description: Goal context ID. Format: gc-<namespace>. Used in identity claims (goal_context.goal_id) and referenced in intent claims (goal_ref).

action_id.description: Action proposal ID. Compatible with AGP-1 action_id format.

agent_id.description: Agent identifier. Compatible with AGP-1 actor.id format for agent type.

principal_id.description: Principal identifier. Prefixed by type: org: (organization), user: (individual), entity: (legal entity).

capability_id.description: Capability identifier. Hierarchical dotted notation (e.g., 'telemetry.query'). Compatible with AGP-1/RFC-0003 capability registry format.

hash_value.description: SHA-256 hash value with algorithm prefix.

confidence_score.description: Self-assessed confidence score. Two decimal places maximum — this is a self-assessment, not a calibrated probability.

result_type.description: Category of expected result. Enumerated to enable machine comparison against actual outcomes.

target_state.description: Description of the post-action state. Must be specific enough to verify against actual outcome.

verifiable_properties.description: Assertions the outcome should satisfy, expressed as verifiable statements (e.g., 'no records modified', 'result set size < 1000'). These are the hooks for outcome-verification defense against intent spoofing (TC-1).

expected_outcome.description: Structured description of the expected result. Structured (not freeform) to enable machine comparison against actual outcomes for intent spoofing detection (INT-040.3).

trigger_type.description: Category of event that prompted the agent to consider this action.

trigger_detail.description: Specific description of the triggering event.

trigger.description: Structured trigger for reasoning. Enumerated trigger_type enables policy pattern matching beyond free-text contains-checks.

extensions.description: Vendor-specific or implementation-specific metadata. AIAM-1 schemas use explicit extensions objects rather than additionalProperties: true to keep the core schema validatable while permitting forward-compatible extension.
